meta.name: Council v2 Developer Test Set
meta.description: 20 developer-focused ideas to validate Council's verdict quality on technical audience post-deploy
meta.purpose: Real-world validation of v2 MVP after deploy

test_cases.idea: Yet another todo app for developers
test_cases.what_we_test: Does Council instantly recognize the most cliched dev project?

test_cases.idea: VS Code rakibi yapmak istiyorum, daha hizli olacak ve AI built-in olacak
test_cases.what_we_test: Recognizes Microsoft + Cursor competition, doesn't fall for 'better X' pitch

test_cases.idea: ChatGPT wrapper that helps you write better Python code
test_cases.what_we_test: Detects 'thin AI wrapper' anti-pattern + Cursor/Copilot competition

test_cases.idea: Blockchain-based GitHub alternative — decentralized code hosting that nobody can take down
test_cases.what_we_test: Recognizes 'tech looking for problem' + GitHub moat

test_cases.idea: CLI tool that takes a screenshot of any website and outputs a matching Tailwind config (colors, spacing, fonts) using vision AI
test_cases.what_we_test: Recognizes specific pain + clear use case, gives GO despite being niche

test_cases.idea: Bug tracker built specifically for solo developers — no projects, no boards, no Jira-style complexity, just a prioritized list with severity tags
test_cases.what_we_test: Recognizes valid niche carve-out from saturated Jira/Linear market

test_cases.idea: Automated changelog generator that turns git commits into release notes using LLM, with customizable templates per project
test_cases.what_we_test: Real dev workflow pain, AI makes it possible, low competition

test_cases.idea: Prompt versioning and A/B testing platform for teams running LLMs in production — like LaunchDarkly but for prompts
test_cases.what_we_test: Hot space, real B2B pain, recognizable analogy. Should GO with confidence.

test_cases.idea: Open source alternative to Vercel for self-hosting Next.js apps
test_cases.what_we_test: Detects 'too broad'  scope (Coolify/SST exist), should suggest niching

test_cases.idea: AI code reviewer that analyzes GitHub pull requests and suggests improvements
test_cases.what_we_test: Crowded space (CodeRabbit, Greptile, Diamond) — should suggest specific angle

test_cases.idea: Slack bot that uses AI to help remote teams be more productive
test_cases.what_we_test: Vague target ('more productive'), should ask for specific job

test_cases.idea: Browser extension that auto-fills web forms on any website using AI
test_cases.what_we_test: Exists (1Password, browser native, AI extensions) — should suggest specific niche

test_cases.idea: Developerlar icin bir SaaS yapmak istiyorum
test_cases.what_we_test: Should refuse to evaluate vague input, low confidence, ask for specifics

test_cases.idea: AI ile bir tool yapacagim ama ne yapacagimi henuz bilmiyorum
test_cases.what_we_test: User explicitly admits no idea — should not hallucinate one

test_cases.idea: Bir uygulama fikrim var ama detayini sonra dusunecegim
test_cases.what_we_test: Refuses to evaluate non-idea, no hallucination

test_cases.idea: Self-hosted GitHub Copilot alternative using local LLMs (Llama 4) — runs on developer's own machine, zero data sent to cloud
test_cases.what_we_test: Real privacy concern, technical feasibility, niche but growing. Should GO but flag enterprise sales.

test_cases.idea: TypeScript-first wrapper for Anthropic SDK with built-in retry logic, observability, and type-safe streaming responses
test_cases.what_we_test: Real but very niche pain (would I really pay?). Vercel AI SDK exists. Should PIVOT to bigger value.

test_cases.idea: Vector database optimized for code embeddings — claims 5x faster search than pgvector for codebases over 1M files
test_cases.what_we_test: Deep tech, real benchmark claim, but Pinecone/Turbopuffer/Qdrant exist. Should challenge differentiation.

test_cases.idea: VSCode extension that turns natural language into shell commands, with safety preview before execution
test_cases.what_we_test: Real terminal pain point, safety angle is differentiator. Should GO with low-medium confidence.

test_cases.idea: Marketplace where developers buy and sell custom GitHub Action templates and CI/CD workflow recipes
test_cases.what_we_test: Marketplace cold-start problem + GitHub Marketplace already exists. Should suggest different model.

steps: Copy 'idea' text from one test case
Note: actual_verdict, confidence_score, first_reason_quality, felt_honest (1-10), would_share (yes/no)
test_protocol.tip: Don't run all 20 in one session — your eyes/judgment will get tired. Do 5 at a time, take breaks.

reporting_format.instructions: After running tests, paste this back to the prompt engineer in chat

template_per_test.red_flags: free text — anything weird, hallucinated, generic, or wrong
template_per_test.surprise: free text — anything that surprised you (good or bad)
